Scenario.title: C0021 Energy Sector Intrusion
Scenario.overview: Energy Complex The C0021 threat actor group is planning a sophisticated attack against energy companies, aiming to cause disruption and gather valuable information.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Shared DB or directory, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shadow IT, Poor implementation of access controls, Authentication issues
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Web, Phishing, Vulnerability exploit, Account takeover, Brute-force attack, Distributed Denial of Service(DDoS)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers gather information about target energy companies' employees, infrastructure, and security measures by scraping public sources like websites, social media, and professional networking platforms.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers develop a convincing spearphishing campaign, tailoring their phishing attempts to specific individuals within the energy sector based on the gathered information.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2010-0188 [CWE-20] : Microsoft Office Word Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 511 - Infiltration of Software Development Environment
stage.description: The attackers initiate access by sending spearphishing emails with malicious attachments exploiting a vulnerability in Microsoft Office Word. When opened, the attachment downloads and executes malware onto the target's system.

Scenario.summary: The C0021 threat actor group plans a multi-stage attack against energy companies, starting with reconnaissance and resource development, followed by initial access via spearphishing, execution of malware, and eventual impact causing disruption of operations. Throughout the attack, they maintain persistence, escalate privileges, evade defenses, move laterally, collect sensitive data, and exfiltrate it using command-and-control channels.
